title: Consonants: Initial
description: Distinguish Vietnamese initial consonants and their sounds

questions.explanation: 'b' in Vietnamese is softer than English 'b', closer to an unaspirated 'p'
'đ' sounds like English 'd'. Vietnamese 'd' sounds like 'z' (North) or 'y' (South)
'g' and 'gh' make the same sound. 'gh' is used before 'e', 'ê', 'i'
'nh' sounds like Spanish 'ñ' — tongue touches the roof of the mouth
'ph' sounds like English 'f'. There is no 'f' letter in Vietnamese

questions.prompt: Which pair sounds the SAME in Northern Vietnamese?
questions.explanation: In Northern dialect, both 'tr' and 'ch' are pronounced as 'ch'. Southern keeps them distinct

questions.prompt: Which pair sounds the SAME in Northern Vietnamese?
questions.explanation: In Northern dialect, both 's' and 'x' are pronounced as 's'. Southern pronounces 's' as 'sh'

questions.prompt: Which THREE letters all sound like 'z' in Northern Vietnamese?
questions.explanation: In Northern Vietnamese, 'd', 'gi', and 'r' all merge to a 'z' sound
nhà = house. 'nh' is a common initial consonant cluster
phở — Vietnam's most famous dish. 'ph' = 'f' sound
học = to study. Vietnamese 'h' is the same as English 'h'
'tr' in Southern Vietnamese is a retroflex sound (tongue curled back). In Northern, it merges with 'ch'
'kh' is an aspirated 'k' — like the 'k' in English 'key' with extra breath
'ng' and 'ngh' make the same velar nasal sound (like 'ng' in 'sing'). 'ngh' is used before 'e', 'ê', 'i'
'qu' = 'kw' sound, as in 'quả' (fruit) = 'kwah'
xin chào = hello. 'x' makes an 's' sound in Vietnamese
'th' is aspirated 't' — like English 'th' in 'Thailand' (NOT like 'the')

questions.prompt: 'c', 'k', and 'q' all represent what basic sound?
questions.explanation: All three are variants of /k/: 'c' before a,o,u,ô,ơ,ư; 'k' before e,ê,i; 'qu' = kw
cảm ơn = thank you. 'c' is used before 'a' (not 'k')
'v' in Vietnamese sounds like English 'v' in Northern dialect, or 'y' in Southern dialect
